image_prompt: SVG: radioactive decay curve of copper-67. X-axis Time / h, 0 to 300, ticks every 50. Y-axis Mass of copper-67 / g, 0 to 100, ticks every 10. Smooth exponential decay starting at 100 g at t=0, passing through 50 g at ~62 h (half-life), ~25 g at ~124 h, falling to ~3-4 g at 300 h.

SVG: four small qualitative graphs labelled A-D, each with y-axis 's' and x-axis 't²', no numeric ticks. A: straight line with positive y-intercept (not through origin). B: straight line through the origin (correct answer for s = ½at²). C: concave-down curve rising then flattening (square-root shape). D: straight line decreasing from upper-left to lower-right.

SVG: four scatter graphs labelled A-D, all with the same six data points and both axes 0 to 6 in m⁻¹ (x = 1/dₒ, y = 1/dᵢ). Data points: (1.43,4.35), (1.67,3.67 anomalous), (2.00,3.87), (2.50,3.33), (3.33,2.63), (5.00,0.95). A: kinked/jagged line through points (not straight), y-intercept ~4.7. B: straight best-fit line through all points including the anomaly, y-intercept ~5.5. C (correct): straight best-fit line ignoring the anomalous point, y-intercept ~5.8. D: concave-down curved line, y-intercept ~4.4.

Mis-crop: the source PNG is a screenshot of the Question 1 stem and the 1a dropdown menus (Select / faster than / slower than... and wavelength options) — no diagram present. Marked text_only so nothing is rendered. Re-crop or drop.

SVG: three panels A, B, C, each a dark cell with a positive plate (top, +) and negative plate (bottom, −) of a parallel-plate field, and a copper-67 nucleus at left emitting a beta particle. A: dashed path travels straight through (no deflection). B: dashed path curves downward toward the negative plate. C (correct): dashed path curves upward toward the positive plate — beta is negative so it is attracted to +.

render_data.title: Beta radiation in an electric field
render_data.expected: Beta particles are electrons (negative), so they are attracted to and deflect upward toward the positive (top) plate — this is image C.

image_prompt: SVG: energy-transformation flow diagram with a draggable label bank (Sound energy, Chemical potential energy, Kinetic energy, Gravitational potential energy). An empty input box branches with two blue arrows to an 'Electrical energy' box and a 'Thermal energy' box; the Electrical energy box arrows on to an empty output box. Student drags Kinetic energy into the input and Chemical potential energy into the output.

render_data.title: Regenerative braking energy chain
render_data.prompt: Fill the two blanks in the regenerative-braking energy chain: Kinetic energy → [blank 1] → Electrical energy → [blank 2] → Thermal energy.

image_prompt: SVG: clean ruled variables-selection table. Rows: Mass of the ball, Time taken, Size of the ball, Distance travelled by the ball, Angle of the slope. Columns: Independent variable, Dependent variable, Control variable, each cell an empty checkbox for the student to tick. Shaded header row.

render_data.title: Galileo's ramp — classify the variables
render_data.prompt: Place each quantity into the correct column: Independent, Dependent, or Control variable.

image_prompt: SVG: three-column data table. Headers: Distance / m, Time / s, (Time)² / (unit blank for student to label). Rows: 0.25,1.02,1.04 | 0.50,1.39,1.93 | 0.75,1.75,3.06 | 1.00,1.98,3.92 | 1.25,2.19,(blank). The last (Time)² cell is left empty for the student to calculate.

SVG: five labelled panels showing the parachute-drop method. Step 1: cut a square of fabric (scissors, dashed cut guide). Step 2: attach strings to the four corners of the canopy. Step 3: tie the strings to a load (mass). Step 4: a student releases the parachute from a fixed height on a raised platform with a railing. Step 5: two parachutes of different canopy sizes descend while the student times the fall.

SVG: optical bench along a horizontal axis. A lit candle (object) at left, a convex (biconvex) lens in the middle, a movable screen (image) at right. Distance dₒ marked between candle and lens, distance dᵢ marked between lens and screen, with double-headed dimension arrows and the labels Candle (object), Convex lens, Movable screen (image).

SVG: three panels A, B, C of a quadcopter with thrust T (up) and weight W (down) force arrows. A: T much longer than W (upward, taking off — net upward force). B: drone tilted with T at an angle and W vertically down. C: T and W equal in length (balanced, hovering at constant velocity).
